title: Stable Bubble Sort with Incorrect Swap

statement.markdown: Stable bubble sort is a variation of bubble sort that preserves order of equal elements. However, the implemented code does not swap elements correctly when they are in the wrong order. Fix the implementation to make it stable.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be an array of integers.
statement.outputFormat: The output should be a sorted array.
statement.notes: The array length will be between 1 and 10,000.

examples.explanation: After applying the sort, the array should be sorted in ascending order.

hints.text: Stable bubble sort preserves order of equal elements. Identify the incorrect swap operation.

editorial.title: Stable Bubble Sort
editorial.intuition: Stable bubble sort iterates through the array until no more swaps are needed.
editorial.bruteForce: The given implementation has a time complexity of O(n^2). Identify the swap operation that causes the instability.
editorial.optimalSolution: Correct the swap operation to make it stable.
